evidence_quotes.quote: terms stating that access can be revoked at any time for any reason
being hidden in complicated legal text.

paragraphs.text: I am concerned that currently there are a lot of companies selling digital media with limited time access. There are three problems with this, as I see it. One; they are selling the media without any information on how long the costumer will have access. This makes it effectively impossible for costumers to determine an accurate value on offer. Two; the media is being sold at a one time charge with the terms stating that access can be revoked at any time for any reason being hidden in complicated legal text. This puts consumers further at a disadvantage. Three; when companies decide to no longer offer access to media, they have little incentive to preserve it for the future. With more and more media being streamed of requiring online connections; this will lead to a loss of culture in the future when these services shut down. Just like historians today look at the dark age and the massive loss in classical literature and historical records it brought, I fear future historians may come to regard our time as the beginning of a digital dark age. Media that was important in reflecting and shaping our current culture is at the risk of being lost because the companies controlling the IP's have several incentives to prevent preservation. I would ask for two things; that measures are taken to ensure both that consumers can easily determine how long they will have access to media before purchasing, and that the preservation of media is seen to after end of service for media requiring services to access.
